items.criterion: File `docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md` exists at exactly that path with non-empty content.
Document contains a Fast Triage SQL section querying `topics` grouped by `publication_status` scoped to `environment='prod'`, matching the `docs/runbook-payments.md` Fast Triage pattern.

Document includes a 'Provisional until Phase 1 lands' banner in §1 that enumerates five TODO(phase-1) items (toggle name + location, suppression mechanism, Branch-B run-row suppression marker, new-editor / legacy bot identity, prod trace-embed channel) AND contains an explicit clause: if Phase 1 ships any mechanism other than Branch A or Branch B (hybrid, per-guild config, per-channel rollout), ABORT to the consult-the-doc escape hatch.

Document's pre-flip preconditions checklist includes 'Phase 1 shipped Branch A or Branch B (not a third mechanism)' as a gate alongside the other gates (Phase 1 soak, schema migration applied, indexes present, backfill parity, lease in place, trace embed wired, legacy editor still running, paged-in window, Phase-1 TODOs resolved).

Document includes a GO / TUNE / ABORT shadow-mode-to-cutover comparison rubric keyed on at least: replay-divergence explainability, collision-override rate, `record_observation` rate, Phase-1 shadow-attempted-publish partials sourced from Phase-1 telemetry (NOT from `topics WHERE environment='dev'`), schema-rejection rate, cost/latency.

Document includes a Publishing-flag-flip procedure with ordered steps, a `<LIVE_EDITOR_PUBLISH_TOGGLE>` placeholder, branch-A/branch-B verification shapes, and explicit anchoring on the next observed `live_update_editor_runs` row (not a fixed 15-minute window).

Document's Branch B flip-verification names the Discord-side count (scroll the live channel since the flip timestamp for posts attributed to the legacy bot identity) as the PRIMARY signal, with any SQL suppression-marker check demoted to a nice-to-have secondary that may not exist if Phase 1 ships no marker.
Document's flip-verification does NOT check for 'zero new rows in legacy live_update_feed_items' (legacy table writes continue during the watch per `:651`).

Document includes four partial-publish detection SQL queries (windowed counts, per-row deep-dive, still-pending-too-long, rejection-rate) scoped to `environment='prod'` with `-- swap to 'dev'` annotations, plus a prod-only `topics` trip-wire count AND an explicit instruction to source the Phase-1 shadow partial count from Phase-1 shadow-mode telemetry (trace embed + shadow logs per `:611-644`), citing the Phase-1 publishing-OFF lines `:611`/`:627`/`:633` for why `topics WHERE environment='dev'` is vacuous.

Document includes a 24-48h watch decision-tree table (SYMPTOM | THRESHOLD | ACTION | POINTER) that maps the partial-publish trip-wire to '(Phase-1 telemetry shadow partials) + (Phase-2 prod partials from topics) cumulative ≥ 2 → ABORT + restore topic_publications' citing `docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:600-601`.

Document's decision tree splits lease symptoms into three distinct rows with distinct actions (new-editor self-contention HOT-FIX, legacy writing to new tables ABORT, new+legacy run-row overlap on same lease key ABORT) AND includes a row mapping 'Phase 1 turns out to be a third mechanism' → ABORT.

Document includes a Rollback procedure (runner-pointer flip) that explicitly leaves `topics` rows untouched, distinguishes pre-rename rollback (trivial — toggle flip) from post-rename rollback (reverse rename SQL), cites `docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:596`, and covers both Branch-A and Branch-B verification on legacy-publish resumption.

Document includes an ordered table-rename migration SQL block that renames all six legacy tables (`live_update_feed_items`, `live_update_watchlist`, `live_update_duplicate_state`, `live_update_editorial_memory`, `live_update_candidates`, `live_update_decisions`) to `_legacy_<name>`, retains `live_update_editor_runs`, runs inside `BEGIN; ... COMMIT;` with a dry-run rollback note, includes row-count parity SELECT before AND after, and a constraint-preservation note for Postgres.

Document names the full migration hand-off chain: author in `.migrations_staging/` (timestamped filename) → copy verbatim into `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` per `.migrations_staging/README.md` → apply via `supabase db push` (or Phase-1 alternate) → verify recorded in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`.

Document's §9 cross-checks the six-table set against `docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:212-223` and `src/common/storage_handler.py:652-1968` — NOT against `docs/live-update-editor-legacy-boundaries.md`'s 'Legacy Storage And Helpers' section.
Document explicitly states that the table rename runs only AFTER the 24-48h watch passes, so rollback during the watch remains a single toggle flip.

Document includes a deletion-schedule section that records the `_legacy_*` deletion 2-4 weeks post-rename as an operator-filled blank (no hardcoded date), plus a final `DROP TABLE _legacy_*` block authored via the same `.migrations_staging/` → `supabase/migrations/` → `supabase db push` hand-off, and named bring-forward / push-out circumstances.
Document references `docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md` for schema shape rather than re-listing column definitions, and points back to it as the ABORT-to-rollback escape hatch in a footer.

Document references `src/features/summarising/live_update_editor.py` (specifically `_publish_accepted_candidates` / `_select_publishable_candidates`) as the legacy fallback path the runner-pointer rollback re-enables.
Document length is roughly comparable to `docs/runbook-payments.md` (~400 lines, give or take 100); not under 200 and not over 700.
Tone matches `docs/runbook-payments.md` — terse, imperative, copy-paste SQL ready, minimal narrative prose.
Every `TODO(phase-1)` placeholder in the doc is clearly labeled so the operator can spot Phase-1-dependent gaps; total of five TODOs as listed in §1.
No code edits outside `docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md`; existing tests still pass (unchanged).

unresolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Plan Step 10.1 names `.migrations_staging/` as the default migration-runner path 'matching the repo convention'. I read .migrations_staging/README.md: that folder is explicitly a HAND-OFF area whose contents must be copied into `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` before they actually run. Glob of the workspace migrations folder confirms it holds the real authoritative migration list. The plan's framing 'default is a new timestamped file in .migrations_staging/' is partially correct (the staging convention exists for live_update_* migrations, e.g. 20260511110349_live_update_environment_split.sql) but omits the hand-off step. An operator running the runbook would write SQL into the staging folder, then need to know the file must be moved to the workspace migrations folder and applied via `supabase db push` or similar before it takes effect. Minor wrinkle in the resolution of FLAG-006.

(2) correctness-1 (vacuous dev arm in cross-window tally): dropped the `topics GROUP BY environment` SQL; §6 now has a prod-only `topics` query plus an explicit instruction to source the Phase-1 shadow partial count from Phase-1 shadow-mode telemetry (trace embed + shadow logs per :611-644), citing the Phase-1 publishing-OFF lines (:611, :627, :633) so the operator does not misread \"0 dev rows\" as \"dev clean.\" §2/§3/§4 reinforce the annotation. (3) correctness-2 (thin Branch B SQL verification): §5 Branch B now names the Discord-side count (scroll the live channel since the flip timestamp for posts attributed to the legacy bot identity) as the PRIMARY signal and demotes any SQL suppression marker to nice-to-have; added a fifth TODO(phase-1) for the new-editor / legacy bot identity distinction. Length budget preserved — all changes are sentence-level edits to existing sections. SD-1..SD-7 settled decisions and doc-only scope preserved.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The new cross-window partial-publish tally in §6 (Step 7.2) queries `topics WHERE publication_status='partial' GROUP BY environment` since the Phase-1 soak start. Verified against the redesign: Phase 1 explicitly runs with publishing OFF (docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:611 'does not publish to Discord', :627 'no Discord sends', :633 'publishing OFF'). A partial-publish row cannot exist in dev because Phase 1 never attempts to publish. The cross-window query therefore returns at most 0 rows for environment='dev' by design — the dev arm of the trip-wire is operationally vacuous. The plan inherits this inconsistency from the redesign's loosely worded :600 ('shadow-mode or prod'), but it bears flagging because the operator running the query will see only prod partials and may wrongly think the dev side is 'clean' when it's actually unmeasurable.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Step 6 Branch B verification depends on a 'suppression marker' column or metadata key on `live_update_editor_runs` that is itself a TODO(phase-1). If Phase 1 ships a publish-call gate without writing any marker on the run row (perfectly plausible — an early-return at `_publish_accepted_candidates` doesn't need to record anything), the SQL-side verification has nothing concrete to check and the operator falls back to scrolling Discord for the legacy bot identity. unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Plan Step 6.5/6.6 verifies via a 15-minute SQL window after toggle flip. Per :633, dev cadence is hourly; Phase 2 likely keeps hourly. If the toggle is flipped between hourly ticks, the 15-minute window may show zero rows for entirely benign reasons (no run fired yet), making 'zero partial' trivially true and giving false confidence. Procedure should sequence verification around the next observed tick rather than a fixed 15-minute window.

unresolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Plan §7 decision tree includes 'Lease contention (both editors writing topics)' as a symptom. Per :651 'Both write to their own tables' — only the new editor writes to `topics`, the legacy editor writes to `live_update_feed_items`. They should not contend over the same row writes. If they share a single `(environment, guild_id, live_channel_id)` lease per :684, they cannot run concurrently at all. As written, the symptom row conflates several distinct failure modes (new-editor self-contention from a stuck run; legacy writing to new tables; lease-key collisions) and gives no clear handle.

unresolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Plan now lists five Phase-1 TODOs (toggle, suppression mechanism, Branch-B marker, bot identity, trace channel). As noted in correctness, the run-source discriminator on `live_update_editor_runs` is implicit but absent from the codebase today and not listed as a TODO — effectively a sixth dependency the operator should not miss. The plan's broader 'all five TODOs resolved' precondition would therefore pass even if Phase 1 forgot the discriminator, leaving the operator unable to run the Branch-A primary signal.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The plan repeatedly references the 'legacy run-source' as a way to distinguish legacy-editor rows from new-editor rows on the shared `live_update_editor_runs` table. §3 precondition says 'latest live_update_editor_runs row under legacy run-source within last hour', and §5 Branch A primary signal says 'no new live_update_editor_runs rows under the legacy run-source after the flip timestamp'. I read src/common/storage_handler.py:652-682 and `live_update_editor_runs` today has columns guild_id, environment, trigger, status, live_channel_id, checkpoint_*, memory_snapshot, duplicate_snapshot, *_count, skipped_reason, error_message, metadata, completed_at — NO `run_source` / `editor_source` / `run_type` column. Per redesign :223/:562 the table is retained for both editors; without a Phase-1-added discriminator (could be a new column, a metadata JSON key, or a trigger-prefix convention), Branch A's SQL verification cannot tell legacy runs apart from new runs and the precondition check cannot confirm 'legacy editor still running'. This gap is not captured in the five TODO(phase-1) markers — it is a sixth Phase-1 dependency.

Per redesign :223/:562 the table is retained for both editors; without a Phase-1-added discriminator (could be a new column, a metadata JSON key, or a trigger-prefix convention), Branch A's SQL verification cannot tell legacy runs apart from new runs and the precondition check cannot confirm 'legacy editor still running'. This gap is not captured in the five TODO(phase-1) markers — it is a sixth Phase-1 dependency.

rationale: Seven of the ten blocking flags are flagged "addressed" by the critique itself and are disputed below with citations to the specific plan revisions that resolved them. The remaining three open flags (`callers`, `scope`, `correctness`) are the same observation under three category labels: the runbook references a "legacy run-source" discriminator on `live_update_editor_runs` that doesn't exist in the schema today — a sixth Phase-1 dependency. These are accept_tradeoff per the brief's explicit "consult the doc" escape-hatch authorization; the plan's structural containment (provisional banner, 'Phase-1 TODOs resolved' precondition, 'third mechanism → ABORT' decision-tree row, default-ABORT footer) already traps the case before any flip, and the executor will add a sixth TODO(phase-1) inline at write time. Iteration Pressure shows zero addressed-then-reopened across all groups; the loop is finding sharper sub-framings, not bouncing fixes back. Time to execute.
signals_assessment: Iteration 3, weighted score 10.5 → 11.5 → 13.5 (rising as the critic enumerates one more Phase-1 dependency per round: TODO count went 4 → 5 → 6). 10 flags resolved across iterations 1-3 with plan deltas 56.1% / 48.1% — the planner is responsive and the runbook's structural shape (Discord-side flip verification, next-tick anchoring, cross-window trip-wire sourced from Phase-1 telemetry, split lease symptoms, full migration hand-off chain, third-mechanism abort clause) is sound. Three open flags are the same observation (run-source discriminator gap) under three labels. Iteration Pressure shows FG-002 / FG-003 / FG-012 at iters=3 with addressed-then-reopened=0 — sharper sub-framings, not stuck churn. Preflight green; no overlap with escalated debt subsystems. Further iteration will yield TODO #7 without changing the doc's structural shape.
warnings: Executor should add a sixth TODO(phase-1) entry to the §1 provisional banner for the legacy/new run-source discriminator on `live_update_editor_runs` (column, metadata JSON key, or trigger-prefix convention — whichever Phase 1 chooses). The §3 'Phase-1 TODOs resolved' precondition then traps the case where Phase 1 forgets it.

Executor should add a one-line note alongside the §5 Branch-A verification: 'requires Phase-1-provided run-source discriminator (sixth TODO); if absent at flip time, treat as third-mechanism case and ABORT.'
Sentence-level edits only at write time. Do NOT add net-new sections — length budget ~400 lines.

settled_decisions.decision: Runbook is doc-only at docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md; no code or tests change.
Rename runs only AFTER the 24-48h watch; pre-rename rollback is a single toggle flip leaving topics rows intact (per redesign.md:596).

Phase-1-dependent unknowns remain as labeled TODO(phase-1) placeholders. The §1 banner enumerates at least six at execute time: toggle name + location, suppression mechanism (Branch A or Branch B), Branch-B suppression marker, new-editor / legacy bot identity, prod trace-embed channel, and legacy/new run-source discriminator on live_update_editor_runs.
settled_decisions.rationale: Brief authorizes 'consult the doc' escape hatch for Phase-1 deviations; enumerating each known dependency traps missed cases without exhaustively branching.

settled_decisions.decision: §5 verification targets Discord-send activity (Branch A: legacy run absent / Branch B: legacy run present + no legacy-bot posts in live channel) and anchors on the next observed live_update_editor_runs row, not a fixed time window.

Partial-publish trip-wire is two-sourced: Phase-1 shadow partials from shadow-mode telemetry (trace embed + shadow logs per :611-644), Phase-2 prod partials from `topics WHERE environment='prod'`. Cumulative ≥2 → ABORT + restore topic_publications. `topics WHERE environment='dev'` is NOT queried (vacuous per :611/:627/:633).

§9 names the full migration chain: author in `.migrations_staging/` (timestamped filename) → copy verbatim to `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` per `.migrations_staging/README.md` → apply via `supabase db push` (or Phase-1 alternate) → verify in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`. §10 DROP uses the same chain.

§9 cross-references docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:212-223 and src/common/storage_handler.py:652-1968 for the six legacy tables; docs/live-update-editor-legacy-boundaries.md is NOT the cross-reference target.

If Phase 1 ships any third mechanism (hybrid, per-guild config, per-channel rollout, partial guild rollout, or missing run-source discriminator), the runbook directs ABORT — it does not branch into coverage for those shapes.
settled_decisions.rationale: Brief authorizes the consult-the-doc escape hatch over detailed branching.

orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.

signals.idea: Produce the Phase 2 prod cutover runbook at docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md for the live-update editor redesign (per the linked v6 redesign doc). Phase 1 will deliver the new editor in dev with publishing OFF; Phase 2 enables publishing, runs new + old editors side-by-side in prod for 24-48h, monitors for partial-publish failures via topics.publication_status='partial', renames old tables to _legacy_*, and schedules deletion in 2-4 weeks. The runbook must specify: exact publishing-flag-flip procedure with pre-flip preconditions, partial-publish detection SQL (the queries oncall would run), rollback procedure (how to flip the runner back to the legacy editor without rewriting state), the comparison-with-shadow-mode rubric (what specific signals from the Phase-1 shadow-mode logs mean cutover is safe versus 'go back and tune'), the table-rename migration SQL with correct ordering and constraint handling, and a decision tree for the 24-48h watch period — what observable symptom triggers what action (abort, hot-fix, accept). Assume Phase 1 lands per spec; deviations get a 'consult the doc' escape hatch, not detailed branching. The runbook is the operator's source of truth during cutover.

unresolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Plan Step 10.1 names `.migrations_staging/` as the default migration-runner path 'matching the repo convention'. I read .migrations_staging/README.md: that folder is explicitly a HAND-OFF area whose contents must be copied into `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` before they actually run. Glob of the workspace migrations folder confirms it holds the real authoritative migration list. The plan's framing 'default is a new timestamped file in .migrations_staging/' is partially correct (the staging convention exists for live_update_* migrations, e.g. 20260511110349_live_update_environment_split.sql) but omits the hand-off step. An operator running the runbook would write SQL into the staging folder, then need to know the file must be moved to the workspace migrations folder and applied via `supabase db push` or similar before it takes effect. Minor wrinkle in the resolution of FLAG-006.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The new cross-window partial-publish tally in §6 (Step 7.2) queries `topics WHERE publication_status='partial' GROUP BY environment` since the Phase-1 soak start. Verified against the redesign: Phase 1 explicitly runs with publishing OFF (docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:611 'does not publish to Discord', :627 'no Discord sends', :633 'publishing OFF'). A partial-publish row cannot exist in dev because Phase 1 never attempts to publish. The cross-window query therefore returns at most 0 rows for environment='dev' by design — the dev arm of the trip-wire is operationally vacuous. The plan inherits this inconsistency from the redesign's loosely worded :600 ('shadow-mode or prod'), but it bears flagging because the operator running the query will see only prod partials and may wrongly think the dev side is 'clean' when it's actually unmeasurable.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Step 6 Branch B verification depends on a 'suppression marker' column or metadata key on `live_update_editor_runs` that is itself a TODO(phase-1). If Phase 1 ships a publish-call gate without writing any marker on the run row (perfectly plausible — an early-return at `_publish_accepted_candidates` doesn't need to record anything), the SQL-side verification has nothing concrete to check and the operator falls back to scrolling Discord for the legacy bot identity. The plan acknowledges this with TODO markers but the resulting verification is thin in that branch.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Plan Step 6.5/6.6 verifies via a 15-minute SQL window after toggle flip. Per :633, dev cadence is hourly; Phase 2 likely keeps hourly. If the toggle is flipped between hourly ticks, the 15-minute window may show zero rows for entirely benign reasons (no run fired yet), making 'zero partial' trivially true and giving false confidence. Procedure should sequence verification around the next observed tick rather than a fixed 15-minute window.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Plan does not describe how the legacy editor's Discord suppression actually flips — it points at `_publish_accepted_candidates` (src/features/summarising/live_update_editor.py:84-107) and `_select_publishable_candidates` (:261-346) as 'the gated legacy code path' but leaves the gating mechanism as `TODO(phase-1)`. Acceptable per the brief's escape-hatch rule, BUT verification Step 6.4 depends on knowing that mechanism — if Phase 1 actually suppresses at scheduler-tick level (legacy editor never runs), then `_select_publishable_candidates` is never reached and the SQL verification on legacy run rows needs a different shape.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Plan §7 decision tree includes 'Lease contention (both editors writing topics)' as a symptom. Per :651 'Both write to their own tables' — only the new editor writes to `topics`, the legacy editor writes to `live_update_feed_items`. They should not contend over the same row writes. If they share a single `(environment, guild_id, live_channel_id)` lease per :684, they cannot run concurrently at all. As written, the symptom row conflates several distinct failure modes (new-editor self-contention from a stuck run; legacy writing to new tables; lease-key collisions) and gives no clear handle.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Verified FLAG-006 fix: §9 step 1 names `.migrations_staging/<timestamp>_live_update_legacy_rename.sql` as the default with a TODO(phase-1) for alternates (Supabase dashboard SQL editor, psql-direct). Confirmed against existing repo files (.migrations_staging/20260511110349_live_update_environment_split.sql and 20260512185328_live_update_watchlist_lifecycle.sql) that this naming convention is in use for live_update_* migrations. As noted in the issue_hints check, the staging folder is a hand-off area for /Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/ — the plan would benefit from naming that destination explicitly so the operator doesn't write SQL into the wrong place and assume it ran.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: The provisional banner in §1 (now five TODOs) plus the precondition gate plus the decision-tree row form a tight containment of Phase-1-not-yet-landed risk. The only residual concern is the unenumerated sixth Phase-1 dependency (run-source discriminator) flagged under correctness; if the operator follows the runbook strictly and Phase 1 omits the discriminator, the gating logic does not catch it.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Plan now lists five Phase-1 TODOs (toggle, suppression mechanism, Branch-B marker, bot identity, trace channel). As noted in correctness, the run-source discriminator on `live_update_editor_runs` is implicit but absent from the codebase today and not listed as a TODO — effectively a sixth dependency the operator should not miss. The plan's broader 'all five TODOs resolved' precondition would therefore pass even if Phase 1 forgot the discriminator, leaving the operator unable to run the Branch-A primary signal.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The plan repeatedly references the 'legacy run-source' as a way to distinguish legacy-editor rows from new-editor rows on the shared `live_update_editor_runs` table. §3 precondition says 'latest live_update_editor_runs row under legacy run-source within last hour', and §5 Branch A primary signal says 'no new live_update_editor_runs rows under the legacy run-source after the flip timestamp'. I read src/common/storage_handler.py:652-682 and `live_update_editor_runs` today has columns guild_id, environment, trigger, status, live_channel_id, checkpoint_*, memory_snapshot, duplicate_snapshot, *_count, skipped_reason, error_message, metadata, completed_at — NO `run_source` / `editor_source` / `run_type` column. Per redesign :223/:562 the table is retained for both editors; without a Phase-1-added discriminator (could be a new column, a metadata JSON key, or a trigger-prefix convention), Branch A's SQL verification cannot tell legacy runs apart from new runs and the precondition check cannot confirm 'legacy editor still running'. This gap is not captured in the five TODO(phase-1) markers — it is a sixth Phase-1 dependency.

resolved_flags.concern: Publishing-flag-flip verification incorrectly checks for 'zero new rows in legacy live_update_feed_items' after flipping the toggle, but per docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:651 both editors continue to write to their own tables during the 24-48h side-by-side — only the legacy editor's Discord send is suppressed. As written, the verification will false-fail every hour and mislead the operator into thinking the flip didn't take. Verification should target Discord-side suppression (legacy run row showing published_count=0, or absence of new live-channel posts attributed to the legacy bot identity) rather than legacy table writes.
resolved_flags.resolution: Addressed all nine open critique flags by (1) replacing §5's incorrect "zero new rows in legacy live_update_feed_items" verification with Discord-send-targeted verification that branches on the Phase-1 suppression mechanism (Branch A scheduler-tick vs. Branch B publish-call); (2) re-anchoring §5 verification on the next observed live_update_editor_runs row instead of a fixed 15-minute window (hourly cadence per :633); (3) reframing the §7 partial-publish trip-wire as "2 cumulative across Phase 1 shadow + Phase 2 watch" per :600, with a corresponding cross-window tally query in §6; (4) splitting §7's "lease contention" row into three distinct symptom rows (new-editor self-contention, legacy writing to new tables, lease-key overlap); (5) correcting §9's cross-reference target from docs/live-update-editor-legacy-boundaries.md (which is about daily_summaries) to docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:212-223 plus src/common/storage_handler.py:652-1968 as the authoritative live runtime surface; (6) naming the default migration-runner path as a new timestamped file in .migrations_staging/ (matching repo convention) with a TODO(phase-1) for alternates; (7) adding a "provisional until Phase 1 lands" banner in §1 enumerating the four TODO(phase-1) items so an operator does not run the procedure prematurely; (8) adding a "Recent runs" Fast Triage one-liner in §2 to support the new verification shape. Preserved all original SD-1..SD-4 settled decisions and the doc-only scope.

resolved_flags.concern: Plan §7 decision tree narrows the redesign's partial-publish trip-wire ('twice during shadow-mode or prod', :600-601) to '≥2 in 24h'. A Phase-1 shadow partial + one prod partial inside the watch window would not trip the plan's rule but satisfies the redesign's intent. Either match the redesign's wider scope or explicitly justify the operational decision to scope to a rolling 24h window.
resolved_flags.resolution: Plan Step 8.1 first row of decision tree vs. docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:600-601

resolved_flags.concern: Plan Step 10.5 instructs the executor to cross-check the six-table rename list against docs/live-update-editor-legacy-boundaries.md's 'Legacy Storage And Helpers' section, but that section enumerates daily_summaries helpers, not the six legacy live_update_* tables. The authoritative current-schema list is docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:212-223 + the storage_handler.py call sites. Repointing this cross-check is a one-line fix.
resolved_flags.resolution: Plan Step 10.5 vs. actual contents of docs/live-update-editor-legacy-boundaries.md:50-65

resolved_flags.concern: Post-flip verification uses a fixed 15-minute SQL window. If the editor runs hourly (per :633) and the toggle is flipped between ticks, 'zero partial in last 15 min' is trivially true with no run having fired — false confidence. Procedure should sequence verification around the next observed hourly tick (e.g., 'wait until the next row appears in live_update_editor_runs, then run the SQL').

Plan §7 lists 'Lease contention (both editors writing topics) → ABORT' but per :651 the legacy editor writes to live_update_feed_items, not topics. The symptom row conflates new-editor self-contention, legacy-writing-to-new-tables (a Phase-1 bug), and lease-key collisions. Sharper articulation needed so the operator knows which to investigate.
resolved_flags.resolution: Plan Step 8.1 lease-contention row vs. docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:651, :684

resolved_flags.concern: Plan does not pick a migration-runner path (raw psql vs. supabase migrations folder vs. dashboard SQL editor) for the §9 rename. Planning question Q4 flagged this; the plan body should either name a chosen runner or carry an explicit TODO(phase-1) marker so the operator does not improvise mid-cutover.
resolved_flags.resolution: Plan Step 10 vs. planning metadata Q4

resolved_flags.concern: Plan Step 10.1 names `.migrations_staging/` as the default migration-runner path but the folder's own README states it is a hand-off staging area whose contents must be copied into `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` to actually run. An operator following the runbook will write SQL into the staging folder and may believe the migration is staged-to-run when it still needs the hand-off step. Plan should either name the workspace-level supabase/migrations/ folder directly or explicitly include the hand-off step in §9.

The new cross-window partial-publish tally in §6 (Step 7.2) queries `topics WHERE publication_status='partial' GROUP BY environment` since the Phase-1 soak start. Per docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:611/:627/:633 Phase 1 explicitly runs with publishing OFF, so the dev arm of the query is operationally vacuous — a partial-publish row cannot exist in dev. The cross-window framing inherits the redesign's loosely worded :600 trip-wire and may mislead the operator into thinking 'zero dev partials' is a positive signal when it's actually unmeasurable. Plan should note that in practice the trip-wire fires on prod partials only, or scope the query to environment='prod' with a comment explaining why.

Step 6 Branch B verification depends on a 'suppression marker' column or metadata key on `live_update_editor_runs` that the plan flags as TODO(phase-1). If Phase 1 ships a publish-call gate that simply early-returns at `_publish_accepted_candidates` without writing any marker on the run row (a likely-shipped pattern), the Branch B SQL verification has nothing concrete to check and the operator falls back to manually scrolling Discord for legacy-bot posts. Plan should call out that this manual-scroll fallback is the realistic Branch-B verification unless Phase 1 deliberately adds a marker.
resolved_flags.resolution: Plan Step 6 step 1 Branch B + Step 3 fast-triage 'Recent runs' one-liner

resolved_flags.concern: Plan's binary Branch-A / Branch-B framing for the suppression mechanism implicitly assumes Phase 1 ships exactly one of those two patterns. A hybrid (e.g., scheduler-tick gate per guild config; publish-call gate with config-driven branch in a different code path) would not cleanly map to either verification shape. The plan's TODO(phase-1) banner contains this somewhat, but a sentence noting 'if Phase 1 ships a hybrid mechanism, consult the redesign doc and abort to rollback' would close the gap.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 3. Weighted score trajectory: 10.5 -> 11.5 -> 13.5. Plan deltas: 56.1%, 48.1%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 10. Open significant flags: 10.

flag_resolutions.evidence: Plan v3 Step 10.1 explicitly names the full migration chain: 'Author the migration as a new timestamped file in `.migrations_staging/` ... Hand off per `.migrations_staging/README.md`: copy the file VERBATIM (filename unchanged so ordering is preserved) into `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/`. Apply via `supabase db push` from the workspace repo ... Verify the migration recorded in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`.' Step 11.2 uses the same chain for DROP TABLE. The hand-off step the critic asked for is in the plan; the critique itself now marks this as status=addressed.

Plan v3 Step 7.2 explicitly replaced the v2 `topics GROUP BY environment` SQL with a prod-only count: 'select count(*) as prod_partials_since_cutover from topics where environment="prod" and publication_status="partial" and last_published_at >= ...' followed by 'Add the Phase-1 shadow-attempted-publish partial count from shadow-mode telemetry (trace embed in the Phase-1 monitoring channel + shadow logs per docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:611-644) — not from topics.' §2/§3/§4 carry the 'dev rows are diagnostic only; no partial can appear in dev during Phase 1 per :611/:627/:633' annotation. The dev-vacuousness misread is closed; critique marks this status=addressed.

Plan v3 Step 6.1 Branch B explicitly names the Discord-side count as the PRIMARY signal: 'primary signal = Discord-side count. Operator scrolls the live Discord channel since the flip timestamp and confirms zero posts attributed to the new-editor bot identity’s legacy counterpart.' The SQL suppression marker is demoted: 'Secondary, nice-to-have ... If Phase 1 ships no marker, Discord-side count is the only signal — that is acceptable.' §1 banner adds the fifth TODO(phase-1) for bot identity to give the Discord-side count a concrete anchor. Critique marks this status=addressed.

Plan v3 Step 6.2.4 explicitly anchors verification on the next observed run: 'Anchor verification on the next observed run, not a fixed window (cadence is hourly per :633). Poll: select run_id, status, completed_at from live_update_editor_runs where environment="prod" and created_at > "<flip timestamp>" order by created_at asc; until a NEW row appears with status="completed".' The fixed 15-minute window is gone. Critique marks this status=addressed.

Plan v3 Step 6.1 splits verification into Branch A (scheduler-tick gate, legacy editor never runs — verify no new legacy run rows) and Branch B (publish-call gate at `_publish_accepted_candidates`/`_select_publishable_candidates`, legacy runs but no-ops its Discord send — verify Discord-side count). The mechanism dependency is named, the verification shape branches on it, and §1 plus §3 plus §7 abort if the mechanism is neither A nor B. Critique marks this status=addressed.

Plan v3 Step 8.1 splits lease symptoms into three distinct rows with distinct actions per redesign.md:684: 'new-editor self-contention → HOT-FIX release lease for (environment, guild_id, live_channel_id) + verify next tick; legacy editor writing to NEW tables (topics, topic_*) → ABORT (isolation broke); new + legacy run rows overlapping in same lease key → ABORT (lease misconfigured).' The conflation is resolved. Critique marks this status=addressed.

Plan v3 Step 10.1 names the absolute destination path: '/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/' (per `.migrations_staging/README.md`), with `supabase db push` as the apply mechanism and `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations` as the verification target. §10 step 2 reuses the same chain for DROP TABLE. Operator cannot write SQL into staging and assume it ran. Critique marks this status=addressed.

flag_resolutions.rationale: The flag itself states 'the provisional banner in §1 (now five TODOs) plus the precondition gate plus the decision-tree row form a tight containment of Phase-1-not-yet-landed risk' — the critic agrees the structural containment is sound. The sole residual is the unenumerated run-source discriminator. Per Warning #1, the executor will add this as TODO #6 at write time, which slots the case into the existing 'Phase-1 TODOs resolved' precondition gate. The brief explicitly authorizes the consult-the-doc escape hatch over exhaustive branching, so the plan need not iterate again to absorb a sentence-level TODO addition.

Same observation as `callers` (missing run-source discriminator). Iteration Pressure shows FG-003 at iters=3 with addressed-then-reopened=0 — the critic is enumerating one more Phase-1 dependency per round (TODO count went 4 → 5 → 6), not bouncing a fix back. Continuing to iterate yields TODO #7 without changing structural shape. The brief's escape-hatch posture (consult the doc, do not improvise) is the structural answer to 'Phase 1 hasn't landed' — the runbook captures it via the provisional banner + precondition gate + third-mechanism ABORT row + default-ABORT footer.

Same observation as `callers` and `scope` (run-source discriminator absent from src/common/storage_handler.py:652-682 today). This is genuinely a sixth Phase-1 dependency; per Warning #1 the executor adds it as TODO #6 to the §1 banner and a one-line note alongside the §5 Branch-A verification at write time. The plan's 'Phase-1 TODOs resolved' precondition gate then traps the case where Phase 1 ships without a discriminator (operator cannot pass the precondition; cutover does not proceed). Structurally identical to the other Phase-1-pending markers; the brief explicitly authorized this handling rather than detailed branching.
